campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary rule (ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Extending temporary ordinance 2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 - grant application and feasibility study approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to various organizations and events in Saratoga. They approved funding for specific groups that had applied for grants, set aside money for a competitive grants program, and clarified the rules for how community events can request money and close streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive secured grant funding
Set aside budget for competitive event grants for the fiscal year
Updated rules and clarifications for Community Event and Street Closure Grant program
Determined individual grant amounts for approved recipients

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, focused on administrative business like approving previous meeting minutes and hearing public comments. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling of upcoming board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact available for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in our city. The commission approved meeting minutes from their previous session and heard presentations from organizations seeking this funding to support local housing and human services initiatives.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding opportunities
Approval of previous meeting records from February 25, 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council met to discuss ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. They covered a business license amnesty program (helping businesses that may have missed licenses), outreach efforts to shopping centers, summer events to promote local shopping, and financial incentives to attract and keep retail stores and startups in the city.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on licensing requirements
Shopping center outreach and engagement efforts
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop locally
Public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships
Financial incentives to attract retail stores and startups to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission Hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed properties or projects related to the city's history and landmarks. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission Hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting via email
Zoom link: https://santaclaraca.zoom.us/j/97233262035

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several administrative items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. They approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities as shelters during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster shelter agreement for city facilities
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Emergency preparedness and disaster response plans
